commit.message:
fix(backend): revert FedEx scraper to minimal Chrome flags for stealth

The centralized config.puppeteer.args included flags like --no-zygote,
--disable-extensions, --memory-pressure-off that changed the browser
fingerprint and triggered Akamai 403 bot detection on FedEx.

Revert to the original minimal flags that worked with stealth plugin:
--no-sandbox, --disable-setuid-sandbox, --disable-dev-shm-usage,
--single-process. These are sufficient for Docker and don't interfere
with puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth anti-detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

coreutils v2.9: sha1sum, sha512sum, join, tsort, tty, logname, printenv, fmt

- sha1sum: pure C SHA-1 (FIPS 180-4), check mode (-c), BSD tag (--tag)
- sha512sum: pure C SHA-512 (FIPS 180-4), same interface as sha1sum
- join: join sorted files on common field (-1/-2/-j/-t/-i/-a/-v/-e/-o)
- tsort: topological sort via Kahn's algorithm with cycle detection
- tty: print terminal name, exit 1 if not a tty (-s silent mode)
- logname: print login name (LOGNAME/USERNAME env, GetUserNameA fallback)
- printenv: print environment variables (all or named, --null/-0)
- fmt: reformat paragraph text (-w width, -s split-only, -u uniform spacing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
research: prove minpoly generalization to K-algebras (cayley-hamilton-minpoly-oq-02-oq-01) (#3407)

Proves that the minimal polynomial is invariant under K-algebra isomorphisms
and injective K-algebra homomorphisms. Builds conjugation AlgEquiv for matrices,
derives similarity result as a one-line corollary of Mathlib's minpoly.algEquiv_eq.

- 0 sorries, 8 theorems proved
- conjAlgEquiv: conjugation A ↦ P⁻¹AP as K-algebra automorphism
- Subalgebra inclusion preserves minpoly
- Composition of conjugations = conjugation by product

1. Hacer fetch a /api/productos-costosos?limit=10 al cambiar a la pestaña

3. Ordenar por precio unitario descendente

fix: drop broken vb_records trigger that referenced non-existent updated_at column

The trigger set_updated_at_and_last_edited() tried to set NEW.updated_at,
but vb_records only has last_edited_at. This caused ALL updates to fail

feat: split album lifecycle into ALBUM_READY state and reorganize state machine into per-operation files

Separate Immich album preparation (PREPARE_ALBUM) from Discord posting
(POST_ALBUM) by introducing an ALBUM_READY state between NOTIFIED and
ALBUM_POSTED. Album creation no longer gates behind the 1h post-event
time guard, allowing retries during the entire NOTIFIED window.

Reorganize guards and actions from two monolithic files into 7
per-operation files under statemachine/operation/, each containing
co-located guard() and action() methods.

commit.message:
feat: auto-upgrade IronClaw binary on Nyx app update

When the Nyx app version changes (e.g. via auto-updater), the startup
hook now automatically upgrades the IronClaw binary from the patched
fork. This ensures existing users pick up critical fixes for orphaned
tool_result messages and parallel tool_result merging without needing
to manually trigger an upgrade.

The upgrade runs in the background during startup — it stops the
daemon, runs `cargo install --force` from the NYX fork, writes a
version stamp to prevent re-upgrading, and restarts the daemon.
On failure it logs a warning and continues with the existing binary.

Bump version to 2.0.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(drilldown): tab context fully authoritative for role text

Replace effectiveSrc/srcEco pattern with switch(tabId). DB source/ecosystem
no longer influences Role in ecosystem text under any code path.

Each tab has exactly one deterministic role template:
github -> open-source project on GitHub
pypi -> Python package
npm -> JavaScript/TypeScript package
docker -> image/container
stackoverflow -> recurring Stack Overflow topic
reddit -> active Reddit topic
hn -> Hacker News stories/discussions
papers -> research topic/term
vagas -> skill/demand signal in job postings
default -> generic pipeline signal (no ecosystem assumption)

Eliminates the docker-image-for-Python-library class of bugs without
any dependency on DB source or ecosystem field content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): add X-Service-Key header and use SDK error types (#26)

## Summary
- **Root cause**: The platform's `/auth/validate-token` endpoint
requires an `X-Service-Key` header. The MCP server never sent it, so
every `verifyAccessToken` call got 401 → interpreted as "token revoked"
→ thrown as plain `Error` → SDK wrapped as generic 500 `"Internal Server
Error"` → Claude Code connection failure.
- Add `mcpServiceKey` to `ElnoraConfig` and pass `X-Service-Key` header
on all platform validation calls (OAuth token verification + API key
validation)
- Use SDK error types (`InvalidTokenError`, `ServerError`) instead of
plain `Error` in `verifyAccessToken` so the SDK's `requireBearerAuth`
middleware returns proper 401/500 responses

## Deployment
Requires `ELNORA_MCP_SERVICE_KEY` env var set in the MCP server's
environment, matching the platform's `ServiceAuth:McpServiceKey` value.

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run build` — no type errors
- [x] `npm test` — all 106 tests pass
- [ ] Deploy with `ELNORA_MCP_SERVICE_KEY` set and verify Claude Code
connects successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Security audit: OWASP-aligned hardening pass (#7)

* security: add security headers middleware and optional TLS to dashboard

- Add securityHeaders() gin middleware: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options,
  Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, Content-Security-Policy
- Add --tls-cert and --tls-key flags to `ry dashboard` for direct HTTPS
- Server auto-detects TLS mode and uses ListenAndServeTLS when configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: fix credential leakage, log redaction, and gitignore gaps

- Improve sanitizeDBError with regex to catch user:pass@host DSN patterns
- Add redactSecrets() to agent log writer — strips API keys, PATs, tokens
  before storing to agent_logs table (sk-*, ghp_*, xoxb-*, AKIA*, Bearer)
- Fix .mcp.json file permissions from 0644 to 0600 (contains DB URLs)
- Add security patterns to .gitignore and ry gitignore: .env, *.pem, *.key
- Harden Dolt init SQL: use least-privilege user scoped to railyard DB only,
  remove WITH GRANT OPTION and *.* wildcard
- Add sslmode parameter to pgvector connection URL in Helm configmap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: add security unit tests for audit findings

- Transport security tests: verify all 5 security headers on responses,
  TLS config field acceptance, scheme detection (11 tests)
- Log sanitization tests: verify redactSecrets strips API keys, PATs,
  Slack tokens, AWS keys, DSN creds, no false positives (8 tests, 27 subtests)
- Misconfiguration tests: verify .mcp.json written with 0600 perms in
  both engine and dispatch packages (6 tests)
- Secrets handling tests: verify DSN regex sanitization, URL-encoded
  passwords, Connect/ConnectAdmin error scrubbing (5 tests, 14 subtests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: fix findings from B1-B6 audit — hardening pass

- Add HTTP server timeouts (ReadHeaderTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout,
  IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes) to prevent slowloris attacks
- Add SSE connection limit (max 50) to prevent resource exhaustion
- Tighten CSP: remove unsafe-inline from script-src, extract inline JS
  to static sse.js file
- Add Gemini/Google API key pattern (AIza*) to log redaction
- Add database name validation (alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen only)
  in DropDatabase/CreateDatabase to prevent SQL injection via backtick
- Add config file permission check: warn if >0600 at load time
- Add env var resolution warning for unset ${VAR} references
- Add prompt injection delimiters: wrap user-controlled content in
  <user-content> tags in engine context template, strip closing tags
  from user input to prevent delimiter escape
- Add security regression tests for all fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: suppress password echo in ry init prompt

Replace promptValue with promptPassword for the Dolt password prompt
in `ry init`. Uses golang.org/x/term.ReadPassword to suppress terminal
echo when stdin is a real TTY. Falls back to line-based reading for
piped input (tests, CI).

Adds tests verifying:
- Piped input fallback works correctly
- Password is not echoed in output buffer
- Default values and whitespace trimming

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix gofmt formatting in security audit files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: fix CSP inline handler compat and SSE admission race

- Move all inline onchange="this.form.submit()" handlers to external
  autosubmit.js using data-autosubmit attributes, keeping script-src
  'self' CSP strict without breaking dashboard filters.
- Make SSE connection admission atomic (Add-then-check instead of
  Load-then-Add) to close TOCTOU race under concurrent bursts.
- Add Strict-Transport-Security header when serving over TLS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: store GA4 OAuth connection in realGA4Client + invalidate queries on property selection

Root cause: OAuth callback stored connection only in oauthConnections Map
and DB, but campaign creation flow's ga4-property endpoint and
ga4-connection-status endpoint also check realGA4Client.connections.
Without it there, the property name lookup and status checks failed.

Changes:
- OAuth callback now also calls realGA4Client.storeManualConnection()
  so the connection is available in all three stores (DB, oauthConnections,
  realGA4Client)
- GA4ConnectionFlow invalidates connected-platforms and check-connection
  queries immediately after property selection (not just after filter save)
  so the badge updates even if user doesn't complete the campaign filter step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle 429 rate-limit with proper retry delay + better UI hints

pageIndex.ts:
- Add getRateLimitDelayMs(): parses "try again in 1.534s" from OpenAI/Anthropic
  429 error messages and returns that delay + 500ms buffer (fallback: 8s).
- llmCall / llmCallWithFinishReason now wait the provider-specified delay on
  rate-limit hits instead of a flat 1s, making retries actually succeed.

demo/src/llm.ts:
- Change default OpenAI model from gpt-4o → gpt-4o-mini (10x higher TPM limit,
  far less likely to hit rate limits during a 32-page PDF indexing run).
- Change default Anthropic model to claude-3-5-haiku (faster + cheaper for demos).

demo/src/App.tsx:
- Add 429-specific error hint: explains rate-limit cause, suggests gpt-4o-mini
  or claude-3-5-haiku, and tells user to click Build again (retry will wait).
- Improve 401 hint regex to also match "Incorrect API key" string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
